Notice Of Motion And Motlon To Compel Verified Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Against Defendant San Francisco Independent Taxi Association Under C.C.P. 2031.010 Et. Seq. And Request For Costs, Sanctions, And Attorneys Fees Under C.C.P. 2023.010 Et. Seq. And 2031.300 Et. Seq. Against Defendant San Francisco Independent Taxi Association, And Its Attorneys Of Record, Joinl Y And Severally, In The Amount Of $1,985.00; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities;
Off Calendar Nov-04-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM videoconference:
David Ebehhack, Esq., Tel. 310.277.7529, for Plaintiff;
Adam P. O'Connor, Esq. (appears after the hearing concluded), Tel. 619.338.0300, for Defendant San Francisco Independent Taxi Association.

Counsel for plaintiff stipulates to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for plaintiff (appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

There being no other appearance at the time this matter is called, PLAINTIFF DANNY BEASLEY's Motion To Compel Verified Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Against Defendant San Francisco Independent Taxi Association Under C.C.P. 2031.010 Et. Seq. And Request For Costs, Sanctions, And Attorneys Fees Under C.C.P. 2023.010 Et. Seq. And 2031.300 Et. Seq. Against Defendant San Francisco Independent Taxi Association, And Its Attorneys Of Record, Jointly And Severally, In The Amount Of $1,985.00 is granted with a modification as to the sanctions. Sanctions in the amount of $1,500.00 are awarded.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling pursuant to 3.1312 and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn, Clerk: J. Alameda, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

After the hearing concluded, Mr. O'Connor appears and notes that he had joined the Law & Motion not Discovery Zoom link. The Court informs Mr. O'Connnor that the matter had concluded and that it cannot entertain any argument. Mr. O'Connor notes that he may file a motion for reconsideration. The Court provides its pending future assignment dates in Dept. 301 for Mr. O'Connor.

There being no other appearance at the time this matter is called, PLAINTIFF DANNY BEASLEY'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have Requests For Admission, Set One, Deemed Admitted Against Defendant San Francisco Independent Taxi Association Under C.C.P. 2033.010 Et. Seq. And Request For Costs, Sanctions, And Attorneys Fees Under C.C.P. 2023.010 Et. Seq. Against Defendant San Francisco Independent Taxi Association, And Its Attorneys Of Record, Jointly And Severally, In The Amount Of $2,260.00 is granted with a modification as to the sanctions. Sanctions in the amount of $1,500.00 are awarded.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling pursuant to 3.1312 and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at williamclynn@gmail.com.
